response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with a 20+ year career, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The mandatory requirement of 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows is not met — his only C++ exposure is approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2007–2008 and a brief mention in a student thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript, and there is no demonstrated experience in fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets systems. SQL is listed as a skill but without any SQL Server or Oracle specifics, and no financial-domain tooling (Summit, Kafka, MQ) appears anywhere in the resume.
strengths: Long professional track record (20+ years) demonstrating sustained software engineering competence
Some C++ exposure at National Instruments writing components for a professional engineering IDE
Strong communication and teaching background evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and course creation
concerns: C++ experience is far below the 5–10 year mandatory threshold — roughly 6 months total, last used in 2008
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge present anywhere in the resume
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB) is entirely orthogonal to the Windows C++/SQL Server/Oracle requirements
